class_schedule.topic: Practicum Orientation and Professional Goal Setting
learning_objectives: Understand the academic requirements and deliverables for the practicum
Set professional development goals aligned with career aspirations
Establish communication protocols with faculty advisor and site supervisor
readings: ACM/IEEE-CS Software Engineering Code of Ethics

lecture_notes.introduction: The industry practicum bridges your academic training and professional career. Your internship is a real job with real expectations. This weekly seminar provides structure for reflecting on your experience and building the professional skills that complement your technical education.
key_concepts: Practicum requirements: minimum 20 hours per week at an approved CS industry site
Weekly journal expectations: structured reflection connecting work to learning
Communication protocol: bi-weekly check-ins with faculty advisor
Professional development goal-setting using the SMART framework
Ethical obligations: NDA compliance, professional conduct, representing the university
lecture_notes.demonstration: Goal-setting workshop: each student drafts three SMART professional development goals and receives peer and faculty feedback
setup_requirements: Digital journal set up and shared with faculty advisor

review.value: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound objectives that transform vague aspirations into trackable targets; 'learn more about backend development' becomes 'complete 3 code reviews on the payments service by week 8'

Non-disclosure agreements are standard in the software industry; all course deliverables must be reviewed for compliance before submission. Describe the type of problem solved without revealing proprietary implementation details

learning_objectives: Navigate the onboarding process at a professional software organization
Adapt to the organization's engineering practices, tools, and culture
Build productive relationships with mentors and colleagues
readings: Fournier, The Manager's Path, Ch. 1 (on being a new engineer)
Google, 'How to Do a Code Review' guide

lecture_notes.introduction: Your first weeks at a company set the tone for your entire practicum. The ability to onboard quickly, ask effective questions, and adapt to existing codebases and workflows is more valuable in the early days than demonstrating technical brilliance.
key_concepts: Onboarding strategies: reading documentation before asking questions, tackling small starter tasks
Asking effective questions: what you tried, what you expected, what happened
Code review etiquette: both as author and reviewer
Engineering culture: sprint ceremonies, on-call rotations, incident response
Building credibility through reliability and communication
lecture_notes.demonstration: Round-table discussion: students share first-week experiences, challenges, and strategies that worked

review.value: Good onboarding programs provide small, well-scoped tasks that teach the codebase and workflow while contributing real value; if your organization does not have these, ask your manager to help identify appropriate first tasks

Reading other engineers' code reviews teaches you the codebase, coding standards, and design philosophy faster than any documentation; request to be added as an observer on reviews for the areas you will work in

class_schedule.topic: Technical Skills in Practice: Bridging Academy and Industry
learning_objectives: Identify gaps between academic training and professional practice
Develop strategies for rapidly learning new tools and codebases on the job
Document technical learning for portfolio development
readings: Nystrom, Game Programming Patterns (introduction on production code)
Selected blog posts on junior engineer effectiveness (provided on LMS)

lecture_notes.introduction: Every practicum student discovers areas where academic training prepared them well and areas where professional practice differs. Legacy codebases, production observability, deployment pipelines, and cross-team coordination are rarely taught in classrooms.
key_concepts: Common academy-to-industry gaps: large codebases, production monitoring, CI/CD, team coordination
Learning strategies for unfamiliar tools and frameworks
Production code standards: readability, testing, observability, backward compatibility
Requesting and incorporating code review feedback productively
Documenting learning for portfolio case studies
lecture_notes.demonstration: Skills gap analysis workshop: each student maps academic skills against practicum role requirements and creates a learning plan
setup_requirements: List of tools and technologies used at practicum site

review.value: Production code must handle errors, log meaningful information, be backward-compatible with existing data, perform under load, and be understandable by the next engineer who reads it; none of these concerns apply to homework assignments

In production, you cannot attach a debugger; you rely on logs, metrics, and traces to understand system behavior. Learning to instrument code and read dashboards is a critical professional skill not taught in most courses

learning_objectives: Assess progress toward professional development goals
Articulate the most significant lessons learned so far
Adjust goals and strategies for the second half of the practicum
readings: Dweck, Mindset, Ch. 3 (on growth mindset in professional contexts)

lecture_notes.introduction: The midpoint is the time to honestly assess where you are, what you have learned, and what to prioritize in the remaining weeks. Adjusting goals based on experience is a sign of growth, not failure.
key_concepts: Goal progress review: measuring against SMART objectives from week 1
Unexpected learning: skills and insights you did not anticipate
Soliciting supervisor feedback: asking specific questions, not general ones
Networking: professional relationships built and their value
Adjusting goals for the second half based on actual experience
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each student presents a 5-minute summary of their practicum experience and adjusted goals

review.value: 'What is one thing I could improve in my code reviews?' is more productive than 'How am I doing?' because it invites actionable feedback rather than polite reassurance

Aim to develop both depth in your assigned area and breadth by shadowing engineers in adjacent areas (frontend if you are backend, infrastructure if you are application); practitioners with T-shaped skills are more valuable and more hireable

learning_objectives: Prepare for and participate in a formal supervisor evaluation
Translate practicum accomplishments into portfolio-ready documentation
Identify transferable skills developed during the practicum

lecture_notes.introduction: Your supervisor evaluation provides external validation of your contributions. Preparing for this evaluation and documenting your work ensures your practicum experience translates into career assets.
key_concepts: Supervisor evaluation preparation: providing context and specific contribution examples
Self-evaluation alignment: comparing self-assessment with supervisor feedback
NDA-compliant portfolio: generalizing proprietary work into demonstrable skills
Transferable skills inventory: communication, debugging, collaboration, system design
Professional profile updates based on practicum experience
lecture_notes.demonstration: Portfolio documentation workshop: students practice writing NDA-compliant case studies of their practicum work

review.value: Describe the type of problem (not the specific product), the technologies and techniques you applied, and the measurable impact of your work without revealing proprietary details or unannounced features
'Improved API response time by 40% by optimizing database queries' is stronger than 'worked on backend performance'; whenever possible, attach numbers to your contributions

learning_objectives: Deliver a professional presentation summarizing the practicum experience
Articulate a post-graduation career strategy informed by practicum experience
Maintain professional relationships after the practicum ends
readings: Review all weekly journal entries for synthesis

lecture_notes.introduction: Your final presentation synthesizes fifteen weeks of professional experience into a narrative. This is practice for interview conversations about your industry experience.
key_concepts: Presentation structure: context, contributions, challenges, growth, career impact
Storytelling: turning daily work into a compelling professional narrative
Quantifying impact where possible
Lessons learned for your next role
Maintaining professional relationships after the practicum
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each student delivers a 10-minute presentation followed by 5 minutes of Q&A
setup_requirements: Thank-you notes drafted for supervisor and colleagues

review.value: Frame your practicum as a growth story: what you knew coming in, what challenged you, how you rose to those challenges, and how the experience shaped your professional direction. This narrative structure works in interviews too

Professional relationships are career assets; a LinkedIn connection request with a personal note, occasional check-ins, and genuine interest in their work maintains relationships for future references, collaborations, or job leads

major_assignments.title: Practicum Plan
major_assignments.description: Develop a structured practicum plan defining professional development goals, communication protocols, and a learning plan for tools and skills required by the role.
objectives: Define three to five SMART professional development goals
Establish communication protocols with supervisor and faculty advisor
Research the organization's products, culture, and technology stack
Create a learning plan for tools and skills needed
deliverables: Practicum plan document (4-5 pages) with SMART goals, communication plan, and learning objectives
Skills gap analysis mapping current abilities against role requirements
Signed practicum agreement and supervisor contact information

criteria: Goals are specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound
Goals reflect genuine professional development aspirations
Goals are informed by research into the organization and role
At least one goal addresses a known skill gap
Skills gap analysis is honest and specific
Learning plan includes concrete resources and timelines
Document is well-written and professionally formatted
Journal entry is reflective and substantive
Well-defined SMART goals with genuine professional development intent
Thorough preparation with organization research and skills analysis
Clear communication plan with all parties

major_assignments.title: Mid-Term Reflection
major_assignments.description: Write a comprehensive reflection assessing progress toward goals, documenting key learnings, and adjusting the plan for the remaining weeks.
objectives: Assess progress toward each SMART goal with evidence
Identify the most significant professional lessons learned
Document unexpected challenges and how they were addressed
Revise goals for the remaining weeks based on experience
deliverables: Reflection essay (5-7 pages) with goal progress, learnings, and revised plan
Updated SMART goals with revisions justified

criteria: Honest assessment of progress toward each goal
Evidence supports all claims of growth
Goals remain achievable in remaining time
Connects daily tasks to professional development
Lessons are generalizable beyond this practicum
Professional insight connecting experience to career
Effective communication through writing and presentation

major_assignments.title: Supervisor Evaluation and Self-Assessment
major_assignments.description: Coordinate the formal supervisor evaluation, complete a self-evaluation, and analyze the alignment between the two assessments.
objectives: Complete self-evaluation using the same criteria
Analyze alignment and gaps between evaluations
Create action items for final weeks

criteria: Supervisor evaluation complete with substantive comments
Submitted on time demonstrating proactive coordination
Identifies areas of alignment and divergence
Explores divergences with curiosity not defensiveness
Complete evaluation package submitted through proactive coordination

major_assignments.title: Final Report and Presentation
major_assignments.description: Produce a comprehensive final report synthesizing the entire practicum experience, including portfolio additions, career impact assessment, and a formal presentation.
objectives: Synthesize the full experience into a professional narrative
Document contributions as NDA-compliant portfolio pieces
Assess the practicum's impact on career direction
Deliver a polished presentation demonstrating communication skills
deliverables: Final report (8-12 pages) covering contributions, growth, challenges, and career impact
At least two NDA-compliant portfolio case studies
Thank-you correspondence for supervisor and key colleagues

criteria: Comprehensive narrative covering the full arc
Challenges discussed honestly with lessons learned
Career impact assessment is grounded in experience
At least two substantive case studies
Comprehensive report synthesizing rich practicum experience

internship_performance.description: Supervisor evaluation of work quality and professionalism

weekly_journals.description: Weekly reflection journals documenting learning and growth

final_report.description: Comprehensive practicum report with portfolio additions

course_policies.attendance: Attendance at the weekly Friday seminar is mandatory. More than 2 unexcused absences will reduce the grade. Practicum site attendance follows the agreed schedule.
course_policies.late_submissions: Weekly journals due every Friday by 5 PM. Late journals penalized 10% per day. Major deadlines are firm.
course_policies.collaboration: All deliverables are individual work reflecting personal experience. Peer feedback during seminar is expected.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All written work must be your own. Practicum work in reports must comply with your NDA.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Students need journal platform access, document editing software, and presentation tools. All deliverables submitted through LMS.
